posts.title: Why You Shouldn't Store LLM API keys in WordPress Database
posts.author: Chris Reynolds
posts.summary: Discusses security risks introduced by WordPress 7.0's new AI Connectors framework and why LLM API keys should be stored using environment variables or dedicated secret management solutions rather than in the WordPress database.

posts.title: Direct GitHub Integration Is Now Generally Available on Pantheon
posts.author: Lucy Hitz
posts.summary: Pantheon launches its GitHub Application as generally available for all Gold, Platinum, and Diamond Workspace customers, enabling automatic code deployment from GitHub repositories to Pantheon environments without manual configuration.

posts.title: Next.js On Pantheon Is Available For All Now
posts.author: Duncan Schouten
posts.summary: Pantheon announces general availability of Next.js hosting for Gold tier customers and above, offering production-ready deployment with built-in performance optimization via Google Cloud Run and enterprise-grade developer workflows.

posts.title: Global CDN Beta: Built-In Bot Protection, Now Available
posts.author: Conor Bauer
posts.summary: Pantheon launches next-generation Global CDN in beta powered by Cloudflare, providing built-in bot protection at no extra cost to ensure legitimate traffic reaches WordPress and Drupal sites.

posts.title: A New Era of WordPress Search: Elasticsearch in Beta Now
posts.author: Scott Buscemi
posts.summary: Pantheon launches Elasticsearch in beta for WordPress sites, delivering enterprise-grade full-text search with relevance ranking, faceted filtering, typo tolerance, and real-time indexing.
